refute.why: The estimator mechanics are quantitatively correct but the *operational* payoff is overstated by the candidate's own framing, and the counterclaims net out to a self-defeating-battery conclusion. Math checks: close-to-close has relative 1-SE noise of ~15% at 21 sessions (95% band approx +/-30%), confirming Sinclair's '30-day HV +/-25%' (p.17-18); range estimators raise effective sample size 5-14x, so Parkinson cuts 1-SE to ~7%, Garman-Klass ~6%, Rogers-Satchell ~5.5%, Yang-Zhang ~4%. So the efficiency claim is REAL in isolation. BUT the decisive counterclaim (Sinclair p.22-23) bounds it: those efficiency gains are largely a Gaussian-GBM simulation artifact, and on real price series the estimators are 0.91-0.99 correlated, so they move together and estimator choice rarely makes or breaks an edge. The bias directions are correct and stable (c2c overestimates via overnight gaps, pure-range RS/GK/Parkinson underestimate because they ignore the gap and assume zero drift / continuous sampling, YZ alone adds the overnight-return term). The 'cap window ~8 months for mean reversion' is a reasonable heuristic consistent with India VIX/VRP mean reversion (VRP AR(1)~0.79, half-life ~3 days; VIX spikes decay within weeks). Net: mechanism holds, but the *return on effort* of running a full battery is low — a single gap-aware estimator (YZ) plus honest error bars captures ~all the benefit. The candidate's strongest sentence is its own mechanism's admission: effort is better spent on the forecast and the catalyst, not estimator selection.

nse.why: This is pure data/computation, not an order-routing strategy, so it sidesteps almost every Indian friction: STT/cost stack, lot sizes, NIFTY-only weeklies, single-stock physical settlement, India VIX untradability all bear on EXECUTION, not on computing RV from OHLC. It is fully codeable in LEAN against the only India dataset QC actually has: TrueData India Equities (~2,053 NSE cash equities, daily+minute, from Jan 2010). The estimator inputs (O/H/L/C) exist for NSE cash equities and the NIFTY index proxy; you do NOT need QC's missing NSE options data to build the RV series. Validated specifics: (a) annualization ~250 NSE sessions vs 252 is third-order — sqrt(252/250) is a 0.4% relative shift = ~0.08 vol points on a 20% vol, an order of magnitude inside the +/-25% estimator noise, so 'use ~248-250 for correctness, but it never decides a trade.' (b) Yang-Zhang is already the de-facto India benchmark: the authoritative VRP measurement in the fact base used YZ realized vol vs Black-76 ATM IV (74.9% positive days, +1.2 vol-pt mean) over 43M one-minute bars — so YZ for gap/earnings-heavy NSE single names is endorsed by the cleanest available India evidence. (c) Microstructure: cap intraday sampling at ~15-30 min on wide-spread NSE names to avoid bid-ask-bounce inflation; with only minute bars on QC/TrueData this is naturally satisfied. CAVEAT/correction: the RV series is an INPUT to a long-vol signal, and the *trade* it feeds still hits the hard wall — QC has no NSE options data and no F&O routing, so any options expression must be custom-data + self-computed Greeks in LEAN, or research-in-QC / execute-via-Kite outside LEAN. So the estimator toolkit is NSE-feasible as research plumbing; it does not by itself produce a tradable position.

regime.why: Estimator choice is regime-sensitive in exactly the way the mechanism predicts, and this is where the (small) edge of caring concentrates. JUMP/EVENT regimes (NSE single-stock earnings, budget/RBI/election days, gap-prone names): pure-range estimators (Parkinson/GK/RS) degrade and bias LOW because they ignore the overnight gap that carries the jump; Yang-Zhang's overnight-return term recovers it, so YZ is the correct default for gap-heavy NSE single names and around scheduled binary events. But under EXTREME jump dominance YZ's efficiency advantage collapses toward c2c (the gap term dominates the range term), so in that limit the 'battery' degenerates to 'just use a gap-aware/c2c number' — which is fine, not broken. CALM/trending regimes: range estimators are most efficient and most trustworthy; the convergence to c2c is tightest. ACCOUNTING/SETTLEMENT regime: if the P&L is hedged close-to-close or settled on a variance/vol/gamma-swap basis, you MUST use close-to-close to match the realized leg you actually earn (Bennett A.2 p.240) — using a range estimator there mis-measures your own payoff. Sizing/gating: treat the RV number as a noisy point estimate, never a precise level — gate any long-vol entry on IV-minus-RV being wider than the +/-25% RV error band PLUS the VRP cushion (India VRP is +1.2 vol pts and inverts ~25% of the time, so a 1-2 vol-pt 'cheapness' signal is inside the noise and should NOT trigger). Use a vol cone (percentile of current RV vs its own history over the regime-appropriate window) rather than a single estimator value for the gate.

net_actionable: In LEAN, build ONE Yang-Zhang realized-vol series (21- and 63-session windows, annualized with 250 NSE sessions) on your NIFTY proxy and gap-prone single names from TrueData minute/daily OHLC; carry a +/-25% error band on it and only act on an IV-vs-RV signal that exceeds that band plus the ~1.2 vol-pt India VRP — do not run a full estimator battery as a decision input, run it once offline only to confirm your estimators are 0.9+ correlated, then commit to YZ.
conditions: Default to Yang-Zhang for all gap/earnings-prone NSE single names and for any name held through scheduled events (budget/RBI/election/earnings); it is the same estimator the authoritative India VRP study uses.
Switch to close-to-close ONLY when your P&L is hedged close-to-close or settled on a variance/vol/gamma-swap basis, so the RV measure matches the leg you actually realize (Bennett A.2 p.240).
Always attach a +/-25% (1.96-SE approx +/-30%) error band to any 30-day RV; never feed a single point estimate into a sizing or entry rule.
Use ~248-250 NSE sessions for annualization for correctness, but treat the 250-vs-252 choice as immaterial (~0.08 vol pts on a 20% vol) — never let it gate a trade.
Cap intraday sampling at 15-30 min on wide-spread NSE names to avoid bid-ask-bounce inflation; minute bars from TrueData already satisfy this.
Cap the lookback window at ~8 months to respect vol mean reversion; for the gate, convert RV to a percentile via a vol cone rather than comparing raw levels.

Run the multi-estimator battery ONCE as a one-off QC validation to confirm 0.91-0.99 cross-correlation on YOUR NSE universe; if confirmed, drop it and spend the saved effort on the forecast and catalyst calendar, not estimator selection.
Remember the RV series is only a research INPUT: the long-vol trade it feeds still needs custom NSE options data + self-computed Greeks in LEAN, or research-in-QC / execute-via-Kite outside LEAN.
nse_qc_implementation: Data: TrueData India Equities on QC (the ONLY native India data — ~2,053 NSE cash equities + Equity Security Master, daily+minute from Jan 2010); use the NIFTY 50 index/most-liquid constituents as the underlying proxy and any F&O single names you watch. There is NO NSE options/F&O data and NO F&O routing on QC, so this candidate is implemented as a research/indicator layer only. Implementation: write a Python indicator/consolidator in LEAN that, per bar, computes close-to-close, Parkinson, Garman-Klass, Rogers-Satchell and Yang-Zhang over rolling 21- and 63-session windows; annualize with sqrt(250). Apply the standard small-sample bias corrections (e.g. RS/GK debiasing, YZ k-weighting). Emit YZ as the production RV plus a +/-25% band and a vol-cone percentile. Validation harness (run once): correlate the five series across your NSE universe; expect 0.91-0.99 — if so, lock to YZ. To turn this into a trade you must leave the native options stack: either (1) ingest custom NSE options data as a custom type, compute IV/Greeks with QuantLib/py_vollib, run delta-hedge logic in code, and attach custom Fee (Rs 20/order + 0.10% sell-side STT + exchange/GST/stamp), Fill and Slippage models; or (2) use QC purely for the RV/IV signal research and execute on NIFTY weekly options (Tuesday expiry, lot 65, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional) via Kite Connect outside LEAN. India VIX is untradable, so the RV vs IV signal is realized against NIFTY ATM option IV, not against a VIX product.
resolved_tension: The principle ('build a battery of estimators') and its mechanism/counterclaims ('estimators are 0.91-0.99 correlated on real data; choice rarely makes/breaks an edge') net out cleanly: the battery's value is one-time and diagnostic, not ongoing and operational. The efficiency math is true in isolation (YZ cuts RV standard error from ~15% to ~4% at 21 sessions) but is bounded by two facts — those gains are partly a GBM-simulation artifact, and real estimators co-move so tightly that the practical residual benefit is small relative to the +/-25% irreducible RV noise and, more importantly, relative to forecast error. The bias structure resolves the estimator CHOICE deterministically rather than by committee: gap/jump names and event-held positions -> Yang-Zhang (only estimator with an overnight-gap term); close-to-close-hedged or swap-settled P&L -> close-to-close (must match the realized leg); everything else -> any range estimator, they converge. So 'keep the toolkit as knowledge, but in production keep ONE estimator (YZ by default), keep the error band, and redirect saved effort to the forecast and the NSE catalyst calendar.' The 250-vs-252 sub-question dissolves as immaterial.

Sinclair, Ch.2 p.17-18 — 30-day HV is a noisy point estimate, +/-25% (confirmed: c2c 1-SE ~15% at 21 sessions, 95% band ~+/-30%)
Bennett, Trading Volatility, A.2 p.237-242 (esp. p.240) — OHLC estimators ranked; YZ for small/gap samples, c2c large-sample; variance/vol/gamma-swap and close-only hedgers MUST use close-to-close

India VRP fact base — authoritative VRP study used Yang-Zhang realized vol vs Black-76 ATM IV (Aug 2022-Mar 2026, 43M 1-min bars; +1.2 vol-pt mean, positive 74.9% of days), endorsing YZ as the India RV benchmark

refute.why: Two claims, both weaker than stated. (1) VOLUME: volume-vol correlation is overwhelmingly CONTEMPORANEOUS (Mixture-of-Distributions Hypothesis) not PREDICTIVE; Sinclair's own takeaway (p.43-45, the candidate's own counterclaim) is that causality is ambiguous and volume is a modest FINAL filter only. A vol spike on surging volume is less likely a single-print artifact, but that does not establish the move will persist enough to overcome theta + the positive VRP you pay as a vol buyer. Clustering (p.37-39) is real but is a property of realized vol of the index/large names and is already priced into option IV — clustering does not by itself create a long-vol edge. (2) FUNDAMENTALS: Sridharan 2012 is US cross-sectional evidence; the candidate correctly frames it as an orthogonal, RELATIVE (which-name-to-prefer), LONG-horizon RANKING signal — i.e. NOT an entry-timing signal and it admits 'long dry spells like value investing.' The high-leverage exception (bond market prices the risk, vol market over-prices it) is a specific, untested-on-NSE finding. Net: directionally sound as a SECONDARY filter / universe-ranking overlay, but overstated if read as a standalone long-vol entry trigger.

nse.why: Asymmetric across the two legs. VOLUME confirmation: fully NSE-feasible and cheap — NSE cash + index volume series are available, ingestible into QC via the TrueData India Equities feed (2,053 NSE cash equities, minute/daily from 2010), and codeable in LEAN as an equity-side filter. This is the strong half. FUNDAMENTAL single-stock screen: severely bounded. (a) Universe: single-stock F&O is only ~220-230 names and the genuinely LIQUID optionable set is far smaller; the Sridharan factors (R&D intensity, cash-flow vol, leverage, ROA) are obtainable for NSE names but the tradable cross-section for RANKING is thin, undercutting a relative-ranking signal that wants breadth. (b) Settlement: ALL single-stock options are PHYSICALLY settled and now MONTHLY-ONLY (no single-stock weeklies); DNE is limited to ~3 CTM strikes. So a retail long-vol expression on a screened single name means holding a monthly option with physical-delivery tail risk and crush from the pre-earnings IV ramp (Indian names crush 35-50% within 24h post-results). (c) Cost: flat Rs 20/order + 18% GST punishes cheap OTM single-stock tails (30%+ round-trip on Rs 2 options); STT 0.10% sell-side. (d) QC: NO native NSE options data and no F&O order routing — option leg is off-platform (Kite/Samco are equity/commodity only), so QC can host the equity-volume filter + fundamental ranking research but NOT backtest or execute the option leg natively. India VIX untradable, so the index long-vol vehicle is NIFTY options regardless. Exact retail expression: use volume confirmation on the INDEX (NIFTY) to gate long-vol entries via NIFTY weekly/monthly options (cash-settled, liquid); use the fundamental screen only as an offline universe-ranking overlay to pick 1-3 single names for occasional monthly long-vol/long-gamma, executed via broker API, not as a systematic engine.

regime.why: As FILTERS (not signals) these reduce false positives but cannot reverse the base-rate headwind: India runs a positive VRP ~74.9% of days (mean ~+1.2 vol pts), so naive long vol bleeds. WORKS: (i) volume-confirmed regime SHIFTS into backwardation/stress (COVID-Mar-2020, Jun-4-2024 election result) where vol clusters and persists — volume surge + IV-term inversion is a genuine long-gamma window; (ii) fundamental high-R&D / earnings-management / volatile-cash-flow names AHEAD of genuine information events where the market under-forecasts idiosyncratic vol. BLEEDS: (i) calm contango ~80-84% of the time — volume noise is high on retail-driven NSE flow and a single-day volume spike does NOT predict persistence, so volume-confirmed entries in calm regimes still pay theta + VRP; (ii) scheduled binary events the market ALREADY prices — Budget day (VIX fell all 15/15 years, avg ~-9%), pre-earnings single-stock IV ramp then crush (implied move > realized) — a fundamental 'high-vol name' screen will steer you straight INTO these crush traps if not gated; (iii) the high-leverage 'over-priced vol' exception is the SHORT-vol side, untested on NSE. Sizing/gating: treat both purely as confirmation overlays on top of a cheapness gate (India VIX percentile / IV-RV spread), never as primary triggers; require BOTH volume confirmation AND a regime that is not a known crush event; cap single-name fundamental-screen risk to small, physically-settled-aware monthly positions; size the index volume-confirmed leg larger because NIFTY is cash-settled, liquid, and the cleaner premium.

net_actionable: Use volume + fundamentals as CONFIRMATION OVERLAYS, never primary triggers: only take a long-vol entry when your cheapness gate (India VIX / IV-RV percentile) already says vol is cheap AND a volume surge + IV-term-structure inversion confirms a genuine regime shift; apply the Sridharan fundamental screen offline only to rank which 1-3 liquid NSE single names are worth occasional monthly long-gamma — and explicitly exclude any name inside its pre-earnings IV-ramp window.
conditions: Demote both to FILTERS layered on a primary cheapness/regime gate (India VIX percentile or IV-RV spread) — they cannot beat the ~74.9%-of-days positive VRP on their own.

Apply volume confirmation primarily on the INDEX (NIFTY) where the long-vol vehicle is liquid and cash-settled; require volume surge to coincide with term-structure inversion (backwardation), not a lone single-day spike, given noisy retail-driven NSE flow.

Use the fundamental (R&D / cash-flow vol / leverage / ROA) screen ONLY as an offline cross-stock universe-ranking overlay on the thin ~liquid single-stock optionable set, not as an entry-timing signal; budget for value-investing-style dry spells.

HARD-EXCLUDE screened single names that are inside their pre-earnings IV-ramp window (Indian names crush 35-50% within 24h post-results; implied move > realized) — the fundamental 'high-vol' screen otherwise walks you into the crush.
Treat the high-leverage 'market over-prices vol' exception as UNVERIFIED on NSE — do not trade the short-vol side of it without separate India-specific validation.

Single-stock long-vol legs are MONTHLY-ONLY and PHYSICALLY settled — size small, manage delivery/DNE (~3 CTM strikes) and avoid cheap far-OTM single-stock tails where flat Rs 20+GST is 30%+ round-trip.
Backtest the equity-volume filter + fundamental ranking in QC on the TrueData India equity feed; execute the option leg off-platform (Kite Connect) since QC has no NSE options data or F&O routing.
nse_qc_implementation: DATA + RESEARCH (QC/LEAN): subscribe to TrueData India Equities (2,053 NSE cash names, minute/daily from 2010) in LEAN. Build the equity-side overlay in code: (a) VOLUME filter — rolling z-score of NIFTY (and constituent) traded volume vs 20-day average; flag 'confirmed' only when volume z > ~2 AND realized-vol pickup AND NIFTY IV term structure is inverted (short-dated > long-dated). India VIX (computed/observed off the NIFTY book) used as the cheapness gate, not traded. (b) FUNDAMENTAL screen — offline, quarterly: rank the liquid single-stock-F&O universe (~liquid subset of the ~220-230 names) on Sridharan-style factors (R&D intensity, cash-flow volatility, leverage, ROA, earnings-management proxies); output a small watchlist of 1-3 under-forecast-vol candidates, explicitly dropping any with results inside the next ~2 weeks. EXECUTION (off QC — no NSE options data, no F&O routing; Kite/Samco are equity/commodity only): INDEX leg — long NIFTY weekly (Tuesday expiry, lot 65 for Jan-2026 series, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional) or monthly straddle/strangle / long gamma when the volume+inversion gate fires; cash-settled, most liquid, cleanest premium. SINGLE-NAME leg — long MONTHLY (no single-stock weeklies) options on the 1-3 screened names, sized small, with explicit physical-settlement / DNE (~3 CTM strikes) management; avoid Rs <5 far-OTM strikes where flat Rs 20 + 18% GST is 13-30%+ round-trip. Cost model in any sizing sheet: Rs 20/order + GST + STT 0.10% sell-side + exchange 0.03553%/side + stamp 0.003% buy-side. Alternative for a fully turnkey backtest of the SAME logic: replicate on QC-native US SPX/SPY/VIX options (AlgoSeek, native chains/Greeks/IV) as a research proxy before committing India capital."
resolved_tension: Claim says volume confirms genuine/persistent vol and fundamentals pick under-priced-vol names; counterclaim (Sinclair's own) says volume causality is ambiguous and fundamentals suffer value-style dry spells. They net out by DEMOTION: both are real but secondary. Volume is contemporaneous, not predictive — valid only as a 'this move is not a single-print artifact' confirmation, best paired with term-structure inversion. Fundamentals are a relative cross-stock RANKING with long horizons — valid for choosing WHICH name, useless for WHEN. Neither overturns the positive-VRP base rate, so neither can be a standalone trigger; layered on top of a cheapness/regime gate they modestly raise the hit rate. The high-leverage over-priced-vol exception is a separate short-vol claim left unverified for NSE.

Sinclair Ch.4 p.60-62 (Sridharan 2012 fundamental under/over-forecast; orthogonal, RELATIVE, long-horizon ranking with value-style dry spells) — ranking not timing

refute.why: Mechanism is mathematically correct and standard. Sharpe puts TOTAL stdev in the denominator, so a positive-skew long-vol curve (frequent small theta bleeds, rare large gains) is penalized precisely when a large WIN inflates sigma; Sortino's downside-deviation denominator (returns below MAR/0) does not penalize the desirable upside variance, so it fairly credits convex payoffs. The peso-problem critique of short-vol Sharpe is empirically backed by the fact base: India VRP is positive ~74.9% of days (so short premium looks smooth/high-Sharpe) but inverts ~25.1% of the time and goes sharply negative INTO shocks (Jun-4-2024: VIX +27.7%, NIFTY -6%) — exactly the hidden left tail Sharpe cannot see. The Sharpe-standard-error claim verifies quantitatively: by Lo (2002) SE(SR)~=sqrt((1+SR^2/2)/n); an ANNUALIZED Sharpe ~1 measured over ~100 trading days has SE ~=1.6 (per-day SR~0.063, per-day SE~0.10, x sqrt(252)), i.e. SE >= the estimate, so a low short-window Sharpe is statistically indistinguishable from zero and should NOT be read as edge decay. CAVEAT (counterclaims bite, do not defeat): Sinclair p.174-175 — Omega/K-ratio are themselves dominated by the same rare tail obs, so the metrics meant to credit convexity are UNSTABLE on small samples; p.177 — scalability beats Sharpe and traders game whatever they are paid on, so the panel is a descriptive diagnostic, not a clean optimization target. Net: claim is right; just don't over-trust ANY single number (including Sortino/K) on a short, tail-driven long-vol record.

nse.why: Best NSE fit of any candidate in the set: a metric panel is pure POST-TRADE P&L analysis with ZERO dependency on the things that break NSE options work on QuantConnect (no native NSE options data, no F&O order routing, India VIX untradable). It computes on any P&L time series — a self-built custom-data NIFTY-options backtest, or a live Zerodha/Samco tradebook exported to CSV. None of the Indian frictions (STT 0.10% sell-side, flat Rs 20/order that swamps cheap tails, Rs 16-17 lakh/lot NIFTY notional, NIFTY-only weeklies now expiring Tuesday, single-stock physical settlement) touch the calculation — they only shape the underlying P&L the metrics then describe. The peso-problem and small-sample caveats are MORE acute in India, not less: India options history is short, NIFTY weekly expiry/lot regime just changed (lots re-based to 65 for Jan-2026; weekly moved to Tuesday Sep-1-2025; BANKNIFTY weeklies dead Nov-2024), and VRP inverts ~25% of the time — so the long-vol Sharpe will look genuinely awful over any normal calm stretch and the panel is exactly what stops the user from killing a working program on a misleading 100-day Sharpe.

regime.why: The panel is regime-AGNOSTIC as a measurement tool but its READING is regime-dependent, which is the point. In calm/contango regimes (VRP positive ~75% of days, India VIX clustering ~13-17 post-2014, term structure upward-sloping) a long-vol book bleeds theta and shows low/negative short-window Sharpe — that is structural, not decay. The rare convex payoff lives in stress/backwardation events (Jun-4-2024 election result, COVID-Mar-2020) where IV inverts and gamma pays; Sortino/Calmar are built to credit those. CAVEAT for sizing/gating: because returns are lumpy and tail-driven, (a) Calmar~=1 is hard to satisfy and lever safely (Sinclair p.180-181), so treat Calmar~1 as a pre-budget — set the return TARGET no higher than the drawdown you can survive, not as a leverage dial; (b) compute the panel on a ROLLING and full-sample basis and require a minimum obs count (ideally including >=1 stress event) before trusting Sortino/K; (c) segment the book long-vol vs short-vol and record per-underlying (Sinclair p.166-167) so a smooth short-vol sleeve cannot mask the long-vol sleeve's true risk profile. Without a stress event in-sample, ALL metrics are uninformative on the tail — gate interpretation on regime coverage, not just sample length.

net_actionable: On every long-vol P&L series (backtest or live Zerodha tradebook), report a 4-metric panel — Sharpe, Sortino (MAR=0), Calmar, and rolling Sharpe with its Lo (2002) standard error SE=sqrt((1+SR^2/2)/n) — NEVER Sharpe alone; pre-commit that a negative short-window (~100-day) Sharpe whose |value| <= its SE is NOT edge decay and does not trigger a stop, and pre-budget the return target so max drawdown gives Calmar ~1.
conditions: Always report the panel (Sharpe + Sortino@MAR=0 + Calmar + rolling-Sharpe-with-SE); treat no single metric as the verdict.

Annualize and attach Lo (2002) SE to every Sharpe: SE(SR)=sqrt((1+SR^2/2)/n). Over ~100 daily obs an annualized Sharpe ~1 has SE ~1.6 — flag any Sharpe with |SR|<=SE as statistically indistinguishable from zero, not as decay.

Do NOT over-trust Sortino/Omega/K on the long-vol book either — they are tail-dominated on small samples (Sinclair p.174-175); require the in-sample window to contain >=1 stress/backwardation event before reading them as performance.
Use Calmar~1 as a pre-trade RETURN-BUDGET (target return <= survivable max drawdown), not as a leverage knob — lumpy long-vol returns resist safe leveraging (Sinclair p.180-181).
Segment the book into long-vol vs short-vol sleeves and record stats PER UNDERLYING (Sinclair p.166-167) so a smooth high-Sharpe short-premium sleeve cannot mask the long-vol sleeve's risk.

Re-baseline P&L series across the Jan-2026 NIFTY lot change (75->65) and the Sep-1-2025 Tuesday-expiry shift so metric comparisons are not corrupted by contract-spec breaks; net costs (STT 0.10% sell, flat Rs 20/order, ~0.8% round-trip ATM but 13-30%+ on cheap tails) BEFORE computing any metric.
nse_qc_implementation: No instrument/strike/expiry needed — this is post-trade analytics, not a trade. DATA PATH: feed any P&L return series into the panel — either (a) a self-built NSE backtest where you ingest custom NIFTY weekly/monthly option records (Tuesday weekly expiry, lot 65 for Jan-2026 series, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional/lot), compute Greeks/IV yourself (QuantLib/py_vollib) and net the full cost stack (STT 0.10% sell-side, NSE txn 0.03553%/side, SEBI 0.0001%, GST 18% on brokerage+txn+SEBI, stamp 0.003% buy-side, flat Rs 20/order) before tabulating returns; or (b) a live Zerodha (Kite Connect) / Samco tradebook CSV export. COMPUTE: per-trade and daily net-of-cost returns -> rolling + full-sample Sharpe (with Lo SE), Sortino (downside deviation vs MAR=0), Calmar (CAGR / max drawdown), K-ratio (slope of log-equity regression / its SE). EXECUTION: none — QC's missing NSE options data and lack of F&O routing are irrelevant here; LEAN can host the research/Statistics layer but Python/pandas on the tradebook is sufficient. If the user wants a clean turnkey-Greeks long-vol book to GENERATE the P&L series, that pivots to QC-native SPX/VIX options — but the metric panel itself applies identically to either book.
resolved_tension: Claim and counterclaims net to 'keep, but as a humble diagnostic not an optimizer.' The claim is correct: Sharpe alone mis-scores positive-skew long vol and flatters peso-problem short vol, so a panel (Sortino credits convex upside; Calmar budgets drawdown; rolling-Sharpe-with-SE prevents misreading noise as decay) is the right frame, and the standard-error math confirms a low short-window Sharpe is uninformative rather than damning. The counterclaims do not refute this — they bound it: the upside-crediting metrics (Sortino/Omega/K) are themselves tail-dominated and unstable on India's short, regime-thin sample, Calmar~1 is hard to lever, and people game any single metric. So the resolution is: use the FULL panel as a descriptive scorecard, gate its interpretation on whether the sample actually contains a stress event, and never let any one number (Sharpe OR Sortino) be a kill/keep trigger by itself."

Sinclair Ch.9 p.172-173 (Sharpe sampling error ~ its own magnitude; verified via Lo 2002 SE=sqrt((1+SR^2/2)/n))
Sinclair Ch.9 p.174-175 (Omega/K dominated by rare tail obs — bounds over-trust on a long-vol book)

refute.why: The mechanism is quantitatively correct and the two counterclaims bound rather than defeat it. Drawdown-dominance is real: a 30% drawdown requires a 42.9% gain to recover, so you genuinely don't get paid recovering lost ground, and multiplicative sizing compounding (Sinclair's same-trades $5,207 vs $620) makes the bleed-budget the dominant survival variable over trade selection. The streak counterclaim VERIFIES the principle rather than refuting it: at a 55% win-rate over ~100 trades, P(at least one 8-long run) ~= 30% (matches Sinclair's ~31%), confirming runs are noise and you must NOT re-size on them. One correction to the framing: that 55%-win example is a SHORT-vol/positive-edge profile; a long-vol book is the mirror image (low win-rate, rare large wins), so its characteristic streak is a long run of LOSSES (bleed days), which makes pre-commitment more important, not less. The second counterclaim (it is fair to change your mind on compelling new evidence) is a legitimate carve-out, not a contradiction: a documented regime shift (e.g. structural VRP collapse, an inversion regime) justifies revisiting parameters, whereas a normal noise-driven bleed run does not. Net: the only thing refuted is over-claiming — the rule is a discipline against noise-reactive re-sizing, not a ban on responding to genuine regime change.

nse.why: Survives Indian frictions, and several frictions sharpen the case. Positive VRP on ~74.9% of days means a naive long-vol/long-gamma book bleeds theta on roughly 70-75% of days, so a long losing streak is the EXPECTED steady state, not a signal — exactly the over-reaction the pre-committed budget guards against; the realistic loss-day frequency answer to the open question is ~70-75%. Chunky lots make sizing binary on a retail account: NIFTY lot 65 (Jan-2026 re-baseline) at an ATM weekly ~Rs100/unit is ~Rs6,500 premium-at-risk per lot, and you cannot trim 15% of a position — you drop a whole lot — so arbitrary size-cuts on a bleed run are crude and destabilising, reinforcing the do-not-over-manage rule. The all-in cost stack confirms the fixed-cost-drag mechanism: flat Rs40 round-trip brokerage + GST is ~0.8% on a liquid ATM but ~30-36% on a cheap far-OTM tail (Rs2/unit), so cutting size into more, smaller orders amplifies fixed-cost bleed. Bounded max-loss helps define the failure budget cleanly ONLY for pure long-premium positions (the premium is the cap); the moment the user runs spreads, calendars, or any short leg this clean cap is lost and the budget must be re-derived from margin/worst-case. India VIX is untradable (futures de facto dead since ~Feb 2016, no options/ETNs), so the budget is a NIFTY-options-premium budget, not a VIX-product budget. Caveat: QuantConnect provides NO NSE options data and no F&O routing, so the pre-commit framework cannot be backtested natively on QC for NSE — it must be coded against custom-imported NIFTY options data (compute your own Greeks/IV, custom FeeModel for STT/brokerage) or the framework is researched on QC-native SPX/SPY/VIX and applied by hand on NSE.

regime.why: This is a regime-robust survival discipline rather than a regime-specific edge, but its BINDING constraint is regime-dependent. The budget is most stressed during long India-VIX mean-reverting calm stretches — VIX clusters ~13-17 in the post-2014 normal regime and crushes reliably after scheduled binary events (15/15 budget days, avg <-9%; RBI run-up bleeds 5-12%), so a long-vol book can bleed continuously for weeks-to-months with VRP-positive days dominating. The payoff is concentrated in fat-tail regimes (general elections: 2024 Lok Sabha VIX +80-191% into the vote then +27.7% result-day spike to 26.74 while NIFTY -6%; COVID/Jun-2024 backwardation shocks). Therefore the failure/liquidation threshold must be sized to survive the LONGEST plausible calm run between tails (size from the worst historical bleed stretch, not the average), and the no-up-size-after-a-tail rule prevents giving back the convex win by over-betting into the subsequent vol crush (post-event IV reliably collapses). Gate: scale gross exposure to India VIX cheapness/term-structure (heavier when VIX is low/contango steep and a known fat-tail event is ahead; lighter when VIX is already elevated and likely to mean-revert). The pre-committed budget itself does not change with regime — only position sizing within it does, and only on documented regime evidence, never on a bleed streak.

net_actionable: Before placing any long-vol trade, write down four fixed numbers and freeze them: (1) max cumulative theta-bleed budget = the rupee loss that liquidates the book, sized to survive your worst historical NIFTY calm stretch (target: at least one full inter-event calm cycle, ~8-12 weeks of ~70-75% loss-days, without breaching); (2) success, (3) respectability, (4) failure P&L levels — then do NOT cut lot count on a normal bleed run and do NOT add lots after a tail win; only revise the four numbers on documented regime evidence (e.g. structural VRP/term-structure shift), never on a streak.
conditions: Clean bounded-loss failure budget applies ONLY to pure long-premium positions (long calls/puts/straddles); re-derive the budget from margin and worst-case loss the moment any short leg (spreads, calendars, ratios) is added.

Size the liquidation threshold from the WORST historical calm/bleed stretch, not the average — with ~70-75% loss-day frequency the budget must absorb multi-week continuous theta bleed before the convex payoff arrives.
Because NIFTY lots are chunky (65 units, Jan-2026) and indivisible, express the budget in whole-lot units and pre-decide the lot count; do not improvise fractional size-cuts mid-bleed.

Avoid funding the budget with very cheap far-OTM tails as the core holding — flat-fee drag is ~30-36% round-trip at ~Rs2/unit; concentrate fewer lots per order or use less-OTM strikes to keep fixed-cost bleed sane.

The only legitimate trigger to revise pre-committed parameters is a documented regime shift (VRP inversion regime, term-structure backwardation, structural cost/lot change), corroborated by India VIX as a gauge — never a run of losing days alone.
nse_qc_implementation: Instrument: NIFTY 50 weekly index options (only index with NSE weeklies post-20-Nov-2024; BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY are monthly-only). Expiry: NIFTY weekly now expires TUESDAY (effective 1 Sep 2025); lot = 65 units (Jan-2026 re-baseline from 75). Strikes: long-vol core via ATM or slightly-OTM straddles/strangles or long single options where max loss = premium (clean failure-budget cap). Avoid <Rs5/unit far-OTM tails as core holdings (fixed-cost drag 13-36%). India VIX is untradable (no futures liquidity since ~Feb 2016, no options/ETNs) — use it only as a cheapness/regime GAUGE off the liquid NIFTY book to gate sizing within the frozen budget. Data + execution path: QuantConnect has NO NSE options data and no F&O order routing (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity-only), so EITHER (a) ingest custom NIFTY options data into LEAN as a custom type, compute Greeks/IV yourself (QuantLib/py_vollib), attach a custom FeeModel modelling Rs20/order brokerage + STT 0.10% sell-side premium (rising to 0.15% from 1 Apr 2026) + NSE 0.03553%/side + SEBI 0.0001% + 18% GST + stamp 0.003% buy-side, and code the bleed-budget/liquidation logic in the algorithm; OR (b) prototype the four-number pre-commit framework on QC-native SPX/SPY/VIX options (full Greeks/IV/fee stack works out-of-box) and apply the discipline by hand on NSE. Execute live on NSE via Kite Connect (Zerodha) outside LEAN. Implement the budget as a hard kill-switch: track cumulative realised+unrealised P&L vs the frozen liquidation threshold and flatten only on breach — not on consecutive loss-days.
resolved_tension: The principle and its counterclaims net out as a single coherent rule: pre-commit the budget and benchmarks, then ignore NOISE and respond only to genuine REGIME EVIDENCE. The 8-win-streak counterclaim (P~31% at 55% over a season — verified) is not a contradiction; it is the same anti-noise logic applied to the win side, and it directly supports the do-not-re-size-on-streaks half of the principle. The change-your-mind-on-new-evidence counterclaim carves out the legitimate exception: a documented structural shift (VRP inversion, term-structure backwardation, lot/cost regime change) is compelling evidence and warrants revisiting parameters, whereas a normal bleed run — which for a long-vol book is the EXPECTED ~70-75%-loss-day steady state — is not. So the tension dissolves into: freeze the numbers, hold through noise, revise only on regime. Both counterclaims strengthen the candidate rather than weakening it.

Sinclair Ch.10 p.201-203 (8-win streak ~31% at 55% win-rate — verified by streak math; streaks are noise)
Sinclair Ch.8 p.131-133 (sizing dominates selection: same trades $5,207 vs $620, multiplicative compounding)
India VRP fact base: positive on ~74.9% of days -> ~70-75% long-vol loss-day frequency; India VIX untradable
NSE F&O structure fact base: NIFTY lot 65 (Jan-2026), Tuesday weekly expiry, NIFTY-only weeklies; QC has no NSE options data / no F&O routing

refute.why: The mechanism rests on three robustly-established behavioral findings and chains them correctly: (1) loss aversion / myopic loss aversion (Benartzi-Thaler) makes the frequent small theta losses on long gamma feel disproportionately worse than the rare large convexity payoff feels good; (2) prospect theory's concave-in-gains value function makes traders snatch small option gains early, truncating exactly the right-tail the long-vol thesis depends on; (3) short-term/single-outcome thinking turns a frequent-bleed strategy into a serial capitulation engine. None of this is overstated for a long-vol book BECAUSE the India fact base independently confirms the bleed is real and frequent, not imagined: positive VRP on ~74.9% of days, mean +1.2 vol pts, so a naive long-vol holder genuinely loses on roughly 3 of 4 days. The two counterclaims BOUND but do not defeat the claim. Sinclair's 'loss aversion isn't all bad - it's WHY options trade / don't buy options for psychological comfort' is a source-of-premium reframing: it warns against the inverse error (buying convexity as an emotional hedge with no vol-point edge), which actually REINFORCES the prescription's demand for a quantified pretrade edge. The narrative-fallacy counterclaim ('a good crash story is not an edge') is the sharpest constraint: it means the pretrade plan is only protective if its 'edge in vol points' line is a real measured cheapness (IV below fair/realized forecast), not a compelling story. So the bias is real and the discipline is the right defense, but the discipline is necessary-not-sufficient: process without a genuine edge just disciplines a losing trade.

nse.why: This is operating discipline layered on a strategy, so it has NO data or execution constraint - it survives every Indian friction by construction, and the India fact base makes it MORE load-bearing than in the US, not less. The frequent-bleed precondition the bias preys on is empirically confirmed and arguably harsher for NSE retail: (a) positive VRP ~74.9% of days means long vol bleeds on ~3 of 4 days; (b) India's average premium (~+1.2 vol pts; ~4.1-pt smaller than US VIX-RV gap) means the long-vol payoff is thinner, so the temptation to flip to the seductive short-vol side is stronger and the convexity edge must be guarded more carefully; (c) the flat-Rs-20/order brokerage punishes cheap far-OTM tails (~30%+ round-trip cost on a Rs-2 option), so an undisciplined trader who buys cheap lottery tickets for 'comfort' (the exact error the loss-aversion counterclaim names) gets taxed twice - by theta and by cost. The pretrade plan must be written in tradable instruments: India VIX is an untradable gauge only (futures dead since ~2016, no options/ETN), so the 'forecast/edge' line is expressed as India-VIX-level/percentile-as-gauge translated into a NIFTY weekly (Tuesday, post-1-Sep-2025) or monthly option/straddle position. The discipline is fully implementable on a Zerodha/broker book; the only thing QC's lack of NSE options data/F&O routing prevents is BACKTESTING the strategy the plan governs - it does not prevent the plan itself, and process-journaling lives in a spreadsheet, not LEAN.

regime.why: The bias and its antidote are regime-universal, but their PAYOFF for a long-vol book is sharply regime-dependent, and the India fact base lets us gate it precisely. Where process-discipline saves you: (1) the persistent calm-regime bleed - positive VRP ~75% of days, VIX mean-reverting to the high-teens, VRP half-life ~3 days - this is the steady drip that triggers capitulation; pre-accepting it is the whole point. (2) Genuine long-vol-paying events: general ELECTIONS inflate IV and pay gamma through the event (2024 Lok Sabha: VIX +80-191% into the vote, then +27.7% to 26.74 on the Jun-4 result-day crash, NIFTY -6%) - here the plan's job is to prevent BOTH early profit-snatching (prospect theory truncating the spike) AND holding past the convexity event. Where the bias is actually CORRECT to flip you to short vol (so the antidote must not become dogmatic long-only stubbornness): scheduled binary crushes - Union Budget (India VIX fell on all 15 budget days 2011-2025, avg >~9%), pre-earnings IV ramp then 35-50% crush on IT names where implied move > realized, and the milder RBI run-up bleed. The caveat: the pretrade-plan's exit-trigger line must encode regime - long convexity only when (a) IV/India-VIX percentile is low/cheap vs a realized-vol forecast AND (b) a fat-tail event (election, unscheduled macro) is the catalyst; otherwise the disciplined process should be telling you NOT to be long vol. Sizing: small, many independent trades, judged as a process distribution - because a single noisy NSE outcome (one budget-day loss, one election-day windfall) is statistically meaningless and over-interpreting it is the very short-term-thinking trap.

net_actionable: Before every long-vol trade, write a 5-line pretrade card - (1) realized-vol forecast vs current NIFTY ATM IV / India-VIX percentile [the vol-point edge], (2) catalyst & why this is a fat-tail not a story, (3) size in lots, (4) bleed budget you pre-accept (expect to be down on ~3 of 4 days), (5) exit triggers (vol-point target, event date, max-loss) - then log only whether you followed the card, and review the process distribution monthly, never a single outcome.
conditions: The pretrade card's 'edge in vol points' line must be a real measured cheapness (IV below a stated realized-vol forecast or low India-VIX percentile), NOT a crash narrative - per the narrative-fallacy counterclaim, a good story is not an edge.

Discipline is necessary but not sufficient: do not use process-journaling to keep holding a long-vol trade that has no vol-point edge; the same plan must be willing to say 'be flat' or 'be short vol' (budget/earnings crush) when the regime says so.

Do not buy convexity for psychological comfort (Sinclair Ch.10 p.200-201) - an emotionally-motivated cheap-tail buy is doubly punished by India's flat Rs-20/order cost (~30%+ round-trip on Rs-2 options) and by theta.
Judge over a distribution of many small independent trades, never a single NSE outcome; one budget-day loss or one election windfall is statistically meaningless.
Express the plan only in tradable instruments: India VIX is a gauge, not tradable - the position is NIFTY weekly (Tuesday expiry, post-1-Sep-2025) or monthly options/straddles on a broker book.
resolved_tension: The claim and counterclaims net out to 'discipline is the right defense, but only for a trade that already has an edge.' The bias (loss aversion + prospect theory + short-term thinking) is real and the India data confirms its trigger - long vol genuinely bleeds ~75% of days - so the pretrade plan and process-based evaluation are the correct antidote. But Sinclair's own counterclaims fence it: loss aversion is the SOURCE of the IV premium (so don't fight it blindly or buy convexity for comfort), and a crash narrative is not an edge (so the plan's vol-point-edge line must be quantified, not a story). The resolution: the antidote is process-discipline GATED on a measured cheapness edge - it disciplines you to hold through the bleed AND, symmetrically, disciplines you to be flat or short when the regime (budget/earnings crush, rich IV) says the short-vol side has the better expectation. Discipline without edge just makes you a patient loser; the two together are the keeper.
citations: Sinclair Ch.10 p.199-212 (short-term thinking, loss aversion, prospect theory; long vol loses most days; summary checklist)
Sinclair Ch.10 p.200-201 & p.208-209 (loss aversion is WHY options trade / don't buy for comfort; narrative fallacy is not an edge - the bounding counterclaims)
Sinclair Ch.14 p.239 & Ch.15 p.249 (pretrade planning most important for long vol; four-part process: goal, edge, capture, sizing)
India VRP fact base: positive VRP ~74.9% of days, mean +1.2 vol pts, ~25% inversion, VRP half-life ~3 days; India VIX mean-reverting high-teens (confirms the frequent-bleed precondition)

India event/regime fact base: 2024 Lok Sabha election VIX +27.7% to 26.74 on Jun-4 (long vol pays); Union Budget VIX fell all 15 days 2011-2025 avg >~9% and IT earnings crush 35-50% (short vol pays) - the regime gate

refute.why: The core claim — concentrate capturable long-vol in liquid NIFTY index options and treat thin single names as event-only — is quantitatively supported and the Sinclair 'product selection + execution > model' thesis survives. NIFTY spreads <0.15% vs single-stock spreads frequently >1-2% (confirmed web + fact base ~1.2%/thin ~10%), and index options carry the cleaner/larger VRP because they embed a correlation/systematic premium single names lack. The pessimistic open question ('NIFTY edge competed away') is REFUTED by data: despite NIFTY being the world's most-contested options book, VRP is still positive on ~74.9% of days at ~+1.2 vol pts — thinner than the US ~4.1-pt gap, but persistent, not arbitraged to zero. The one genuine bound is on the Bennett 2.1 counterclaim that long gamma can HARVEST the wide single-stock spread via limit orders: that logic is correct in the abstract and actually favors a buying-only retail trader, BUT on NSE it is defeated for single names by structural frictions Bennett's frame omits — physical settlement (delivery risk), monthly-only expiry (no weekly gamma to scalp), and flat Rs 40 round-trip brokerage that swamps cheap tails (~30%+ of a Rs 2 option). So the spread-harvest edge is real but NOT a reason to favor thin single names; it is a reason a NIFTY buyer should always work limit orders. Net: mechanism holds; the claim/counterclaim tension resolves cleanly toward NIFTY for a retail buyer rather than being defeated.

nse.why: Survives Indian frictions but the binding constraint is the platform, not instrument choice — exactly as the prelim fit states. NIFTY is the only retail-accessible clean long-gamma/long-vega venue: weekly options on NIFTY 50 ONLY (SEBI one-weekly-per-exchange; BANKNIFTY/FINNIFTY/MIDCPNIFTY weeklies discontinued, last BANKNIFTY weekly Nov-13-2024), weekly expiry now TUESDAY (eff Sep-1-2025), lot 65 (Jan-2026 re-baseline, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional), cash-settled, paise spreads, round-trip ~0.8% on a liquid ATM weekly. Single-stock options ARE viable as event-only/hold-to-payoff vehicles but only for the ~220-230 F&O names with real depth (large-cap: Reliance, HDFC Bank, ICICI, Infosys, TCS), and they carry two retail-hostile frictions: PHYSICAL settlement (compulsory delivery — must square off before expiry or use the narrow ~3-strike DNE band) and MONTHLY-only expiry (no weekly gamma). India VIX is untradable (futures de facto dead since ~Feb 2016, no options/ETN) so direct long-vega via VIX is impossible — long vol MUST come from NIFTY/stock options, which the candidate already assumes. The hard wall: QuantConnect has NO native NSE options data and NO F&O order routing (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity only). So the EXACT retail-accessible expression is: NIFTY weekly/monthly options bought via a broker API (Kite Connect) with disciplined limit orders; QC is usable only for signal/research on custom-imported data (roll-your-own Greeks/fees) or — cleaner — pivot the engine work to QC-native US SPX/SPY/VIX and port the logic. There is no native QC path to trade NSE options.

regime.why: Instrument selection is regime-robust as a STANDING rule (always prefer NIFTY for the core; always work limits), but the long-vol PAYOFF the instruments deliver is regime-dependent and that gates sizing. NIFTY long gamma/vega bleeds in calm/recovery (VRP largest then, ~+1.2 pts, theta + vega decay) and in the ~75% of days IV>RV — this is the dominant drag. It PAYS in genuine fat-tail regimes: unscheduled shocks, term-structure inversion/backwardation (COVID-Mar-2020, Jun-4-2024 election result), and the ~25% of days VRP inverts. CRITICAL event asymmetry to gate on: scheduled binary events (Union Budget, RBI policy) reliably CRUSH vol (15/15 budget days VIX down ~9% avg) — long straddles LOSE through them; while genuine-uncertainty events (general elections) INFLATE vol and pay long vol (2024 VIX +80-191% into vote, +27.7% on Jun-4 result). Single-stock earnings show the same crush trap (implied move > realized, buy-and-hold straddle loses), so the 'event-only single name' must be reserved for genuine-uncertainty/fat-tail catalysts, NOT scheduled crushes. Robust sizing: keep the NIFTY long-gamma core small and continuously financed (sell theta-rich short-dated against long-dated, or buy only when India VIX is in the cheap ~13-15 lower regime given strong mean-reversion, half-life ~weeks); gate single-name long vol to confirmed long-vol catalysts only, sized as defined-risk hold-to-payoff with square-off before physical settlement.

net_actionable: Build the long-vol core ONLY in NIFTY weekly/monthly index options (cash-settled, Tuesday weekly expiry, lot 65), always entered with passive limit orders resting on bid/offer; use single-stock options exclusively as defined-risk, hold-to-payoff plays on genuine-uncertainty catalysts among the top ~10-15 liquid large-cap F&O names, never through scheduled vol-crush events (Budget/RBI/earnings), and always squared off before monthly expiry to avoid physical delivery.
conditions: NIFTY index options are the only venue for the long-gamma/long-vega core and short-dated weekly gamma — do not put core long-vol in single stocks.
Always execute via passive limit orders sitting on bid/offer (buying-only retail can harvest spread rather than pay it); never cross the spread for the long-gamma hedge.

Single-stock long vol is event-only and restricted to large-cap F&O names with real depth (spread <~1%); avoid the truly thin niches whose 'rich mispricing' is unrecoverable after flat-brokerage + physical-settlement + monthly-only frictions.

Never hold long vol through a scheduled vol-crush event (Union Budget, RBI policy, single-stock earnings) — reserve long-vol event plays for genuine-uncertainty catalysts (elections, unscheduled shocks).
Square off all single-stock option positions before expiry — physical settlement means assignment = compulsory delivery; DNE covers only ~3 CTM strikes and not at all for index (cash-settled).

Trading is via broker API (Kite Connect); QuantConnect cannot route NSE F&O and has no NSE options data — use QC only for research/signal on custom data, or port engine work to QC-native US SPX/SPY/VIX.
Size the always-bleeding NIFTY core small and prefer entry when India VIX sits in the cheap ~13-15 regime (strong mean-reversion); avoid initiating long vega when VIX is already elevated.
resolved_tension: Sinclair's own counterclaim (richest mispricings live where competition is thin) and Bennett 2.1 (wide illiquid spread can be harvested by long gamma via limit orders) initially pull toward the thin NSE single names. They net out AGAINST that pull for THIS retail buyer for three reasons the original sources don't fully internalize for the NSE microstructure: (1) the spread-harvest logic — correct and pro-buyer — applies just as well to NIFTY, so it argues for limit-order discipline, not for switching venues; (2) NSE thin single names carry physical settlement, monthly-only expiry, and a flat-brokerage tax that makes the 'rich' tail mispricing structurally unrecoverable, frictions absent from Bennett's frame; (3) the index VRP is empirically still alive (+1.2 vol pts, 75% of days) despite heavy professional competition, so the feared 'edge competed away' is thinner-but-real, not gone. The tension therefore resolves to: NIFTY for the capturable core (clean execution + persistent if modest edge), single names demoted to occasional event-only hold-to-payoff vehicles — exactly the candidate's split. The 'product + execution > model' meta-claim survives strengthened, because on NSE the dominant determinant is in fact the platform/data constraint (QC can't even trade these), which sits one level above instrument choice.
citations: Sinclair Ch.15 p.251-252 (product selection biggest factor; execution often the most important skill) — survives and is reinforced by NSE platform constraint
Bennett 1.2 p.7 (index options liquid long-dated; single stocks fade after 1yr; OTM more liquid than ITM) — matches NSE NIFTY-vs-single-name liquidity stratification
VERIFIED FACT BASE: NSE F&O structure (NIFTY-only weeklies, Tuesday expiry eff Sep-1-2025, lot 65 Jan-2026, single-stock physical settlement + monthly-only, DNE narrow band)
VERIFIED FACT BASE: cost stack (flat Rs 40 round-trip swamps cheap tails ~30%+; liquid ATM ~0.8%) + India VRP positive ~74.9% of days at ~+1.2 vol pts (edge persistent, not competed away)

VERIFIED FACT BASE + web confirmation: QuantConnect has NO NSE options data and NO F&O routing; NIFTY spreads <0.15% vs single-stock >1-2%; India VIX untradable — long vol must come from NIFTY/stock options

refute.why: Every load-bearing claim is factually confirmed by the verified fact base, and the mechanism is not overstated. (1) Data gap: QC's ONLY India dataset is TrueData 'India Equities' (~2,053 NSE cash equities, daily/minute from Jan 2010) plus an Equity Security Master — zero NSE options/index-options/F&O-futures history, despite TrueData itself being an authorized NSE F&O vendor (a licensing, not technical, block). (2) Execution gap: both Indian brokerage plugins (Zerodha Kite Connect, Samco) route NSE/BSE EQUITY + MCX COMMODITY only (MIS/CNC/NRML); neither plugin documents F&O/options order routing, so there is no native NSE options execution path. (3) Custom-data does NOT recover the turnkey stack: custom-imported option records are not registered as native Option securities, so AddOptionContract throws KeyErrors, contracts can't be ordered through the native pipeline, and Greeks aren't auto-computed; QC staff (Louis Szeto) confirmed only partial hacks (Symbol.CreateOption, per-contract custom-data classes) and did NOT confirm native Greeks/fill treatment. The counterclaim is real but BOUNDED — LEAN's pricing models (BlackScholes/BinomialCoxRossRubinstein/ForwardTree), fee/fill models, and code-driven delta-hedging via SetHoldings are genuinely solid and exchange-agnostic — but that only saves the MODELING layer if you self-source data and re-implement Greeks/hedging in code; it does not give you a turnkey NSE chain. So the counterclaim does not defeat the principle; it precisely scopes it. The only nuance worth flagging: the principle's framing that QC is 'at best a research/modeling engine for NSE' is correct but slightly understates that even the modeling layer requires non-trivial DIY (compute IV via QuantLib/py_vollib, custom FeeModel for Rs 20 + STT 0.10% sell-side + charges, custom FillModel). Mechanism is correct and not overstated.

nse.why: This candidate IS the NSE-applicability verdict for the entire long-vol-on-NSE program, and it survives every Indian friction by correctly identifying them as the binding constraint. NSE frictions don't refute it — they are exactly why the turnkey-QC ambition fails and force the recast. Concretely: (a) the ONLY retail-accessible NSE long-vol expression is NIFTY index options (cash-settled, NIFTY-only weeklies on TUESDAY since 1 Sep 2025, lot 65 for Jan-2026 series, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional) or single-stock options (physically settled, monthly-only) — and QC can model NEITHER natively; (b) the cost stack QC must replicate via a custom FeeModel is Rs 20/order + STT 0.10% sell-side + NSE 0.03553%/side + SEBI 0.0001%/side + GST 18% on (brokerage+exch+SEBI) + stamp 0.003% buy-side — non-trivial and material on cheap tails (~30%+ round-trip on a Rs 2 OTM); (c) India VIX is untradable (futures de-facto dead since ~Feb 2016, no options/ETFs/ETNs) so there is no vol-product shortcut that would sidestep the options-data requirement. The EXACT retail-accessible NSE expression for backtesting on QC: NONE turnkey — you must ingest custom NSE options + underlying history into LEAN, mint symbols via Symbol.CreateOption or per-contract custom-data classes, compute Greeks/IV yourself, attach a custom FeeModel/FillModel, and run delta-hedge logic in code; live execution must go through a broker API (Kite Connect) OUTSIDE LEAN, with QC's native execution usable only for the equity hedge leg. The clean turnkey alternative exists only off-NSE: QC-native AlgoSeek US Index Options (SPX/SPXW/VIX/VIXW/NDX/RUT, European, from Jan 2012) and US Equity Options (incl. SPY, from Jan 2012) with a pre-computed Greeks/IV universe.

regime.why: This is an infrastructure/instrument-selection constraint, not a market-regime edge, so 'works vs bleeds by regime' maps to 'which development path pays off given the trader's goals' rather than vol regimes. It HOLDS as a hard constraint in all market regimes — there is no regime in which QC suddenly ships NSE options data or routes F&O. The caveat is on the DECISION it forces: (1) If the trader's edge thesis is NSE-specific (Budget-day VIX crush, Jun-4-2024-style election fat-tail long-vol, NIFTY weekly/0DTE Tuesday-expiry gamma, BANKNIFTY-monthly post-discontinuation dynamics) then the DIY NSE data+execution path is unavoidable and the QC value is confined to modeling/research + the equity hedge leg — accept the build cost. refute.why: Split the mechanism in two. (A) STRUCTURAL-BREAK half holds strongly and is quantified: the SEBI 2024-25 overhaul is a textbook backtest-invalidating regime break — NIFTY lot 25->75 (Nov-2024) then re-baselined ->65 (Jan-2026), BANKNIFTY weeklies killed (last weekly Nov-13-2024, now monthly-only), one-weekly-per-exchange leaving NIFTY-only weeklies, Thursday->Tuesday expiry (Sep-1-2025), sell-side options STT 0.0625%->0.10% (Oct-1-2024) and ->0.15% (Apr-1-2026). Each materially changes notional, gamma microstructure and cost — pre-2024 long-vol/expiry-gamma edge estimates are simply not in-regime. (B) CROWDING/'edge competed away' half is partly BACKWARDS for a long-vol BUYER. The fact base shows India runs a POSITIVE VRP (IV>RV on ~74.9% of days, mean +1.2 vol pts), so it is vol SELLERS who are paid; long vol structurally BLEEDS. 'Crowding' of vol-selling does not erode a long-vol edge — it would RICHEN premia and WIDEN long-vol bleed. The candidate conflates the eroding vol-selling alpha (Bennett 3.1) with a long-vol edge that doesn't exist as a carry edge. The disciplined truth, and the genuine good news, is the MIRROR counterclaim: India's VRP gap (+1.2 vol pts) is far smaller than the US (~4.1 vol pts), so long-vol bleed in NIFTY is structurally cheaper than US-trained intuition or US backtests imply — degradation can be illusory (Sinclair p.184). Net: monitoring discipline is correct; the specific 'long-vol edge is being competed away' phrasing must be recast to 'monitor whether the VRP I pay is richening vs the historical +1.2, and re-estimate after every SEBI break.'

nse.why: This is the candidate's strongest lens — it is MORE applicable to NSE retail than the source books assume, because India's instrument set is uniquely restricted, making the 'reframe book strategies' core of the insight mandatory rather than optional. Every reframing prescription checks out against the fact base: (1) Var swap -> 1/K^2 NIFTY option strip (Bennett A.4) is the only var-exposure path since OTC var swaps are unavailable to retail. (2) VIX-long -> NIFTY straddles is FORCED, not a preference: India VIX futures are de facto dead (one transaction FY2016-17, last trade ~Feb-2016) and no India VIX options/ETF/ETN ever listed — India VIX is a GAUGE only, never tradable. (3) Dispersion -> long single-stock straddles vs short NIFTY is feasible only within the ~220-230 stock F&O universe and is bounded by single-stock options being MONTHLY-only with PHYSICAL settlement (DNE limited to ~3 CTM strikes) plus shallower single-stock skew and smaller/noisier single-name VRP — so it is costly and liquidity-gated, not a clean retail trade. (4) The QC reinforcement is decisive: QC provides NO NSE options/F&O data and no F&O order routing (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity only), so ANY pre-2024 NSE long-vol backtest is doubly invalid — wrong regime AND not even natively backtestable. The frictions (flat Rs20/order brokerage punishing cheap tails — ~30%+ round-trip on a Rs2 OTM, STT on sell-side, ~Rs16-17 lakh/lot notional) bound position selection but do not defeat the diagnostic discipline.

regime.why: This is a meta/diagnostic discipline, so it 'works' in every regime by construction; the value is in defining the gates. Two regime-classes matter. (1) DISCRETE structural-break events (SEBI circulars: lot resize, weekly cuts, expiry-day shift, STT changes, margin rule changes) are step-function 're-estimate everything' triggers — treat any backtest spanning a break date as out-of-regime and refit on post-break data only. (2) CONTINUOUS edge-monitoring: track the realized VRP (NIFTY ATM IV minus subsequent Yang-Zhang RV) against the +1.2 vol-pt baseline and the ~25% inversion rate. Long vol PAYS in the inversion/shock regime (VRP goes negative going INTO shocks — COVID-Mar-2020, Jun-4-2024 election surprise where VIX +27.7% as NIFTY -6%) and in genuine fat-tail events (elections inflate IV through the event). Long vol BLEEDS in the calm/recovery regime and through scheduled binary events that crush (Budget day: India VIX fell all 15/15 days 2011-2025, avg >-9%; IT earnings crush ATM IV 35-50% in 24h). Sizing/gating: gate long-vol entry on cheapness vs the mean-reverting VIX band (~13-17 post-2014 normal), AR(1)~0.79 / ~3-day premium half-life means don't chase; carry small persistent long-gamma sized to survive the bleed, scale up only when VRP compresses toward/through zero or ahead of true fat-tails (elections), and AVOID holding long premium through scheduled crush events (Budget, single-stock earnings).

net_actionable: Maintain a one-page 'regime log': (a) on every SEBI/NSE structural circular, stamp the date and discard any NIFTY long-vol stat estimated across it — refit only on post-break data; (b) weekly, log realized NIFTY VRP (ATM IV minus 20-day Yang-Zhang RV) against the +1.2 vol-pt baseline, and only add persistent long-gamma when that premium compresses toward/through zero (cheap), never through a scheduled crush event (Budget/earnings).

refute.why: The replication math is textbook-correct: w(K)=const/K^2 gives constant dollar-gamma across spot (the log-contract), and variance > gamma swap > vol swap by Jensen — the quadratic payoff genuinely pays a convex tail bonus on large moves, which is the long-vol trader's prize. But the candidate OVERSTATES practical benefit and is bounded hard by its own counterclaims. (1) Truncation kills the thesis: the convex tail bonus lives in the deep-OTM wings (Bennett 2.3 p.54 — not listed/liquid), so a truncated strip under-replicates exactly the large-move convexity it is sold for. (2) The theorem assumes CONTINUOUS delta-hedging (Sinclair p.225); discrete daily hedging injects gamma-scaled P&L noise. (3) Variance is bounded below at 0, max loss = sigma_strike*vega/2 on a vol collapse (Bennett 2.3 p.66), and against India's positive VRP (~75% of days IV>RV) a static long-variance strip is a structural negative-carry position. Mechanism: correct. Net retail edge: materially smaller than advertised because the buyable strip is precisely the strip stripped of its tail convexity.

nse.why: An exact retail expression EXISTS but is severely degraded; honest recast required. Rationale chain: India VIX is untradable (dead futures, no options/ETFs) and no NSE variance swaps are listed, so a DIY 1/K^2 NIFTY option strip + futures delta-hedge is genuinely the only model-free variance route — feasible only on NIFTY (densest chain; weekly options NIFTY-only, Tuesday expiry since 1-Sep-2025; BANKNIFTY monthly-only). Three frictions cripple the full strip: (a) COST — flat Rs 20/order brokerage (Rs 40 RT + ~Rs 7 GST) is charged PER STRIKE PER SIDE; cheap far-OTM puts cost 13% at Rs 5/unit and 30%+ at Rs 2/unit, so the wing legs that carry the convex bonus are exactly the legs the fixed cost destroys — a 15-20 strike strip pays Rs 300-400/side in brokerage alone before STT (0.10% sell-side) and spreads. (b) LOT QUANTIZATION — NIFTY lot 65 (Jan-2026, ~Rs 16-17 lakh notional) forces integer-lot legs, so a small account cannot finely 1/K^2-weight; the weighting is badly distorted. (c) DELTA HEDGE needs NIFTY futures (~Rs 16-17 lakh/lot) and daily rebalancing. On QC specifically: NO NSE options/F&O data and no F&O order routing (Zerodha/Samco plugins are equity/commodity-only), so the strip cannot be backtested natively — it requires a full custom-data layer (self-computed Greeks/IV via QuantLib/py_vollib + custom FeeModel/FillModel) and live execution via Kite OUTSIDE LEAN. Net: a TRUE variance swap is not buildable at retail; what is buildable is a truncated, lot-quantized, put-heavy approximation.

regime.why: Strongly regime-dependent. WORKS (and beats a single straddle) in large-move / fat-tail regimes where constant dollar-gamma keeps paying as spot crashes far from any one strike: general elections (Jun-4-2024 result day India VIX +27.7% to 26.74, NIFTY ~-6% — textbook long-vol payoff for held gamma), COVID-style crashes, and term-structure backwardation onset. This is its genuine edge over a strike-anchored straddle. BLEEDS the rest of the time, which is most of the time: India VRP is positive ~75% of days, India VIX mean-reverts fast (VRP AR1~0.79, half-life ~3 days; VIX spikes decay within weeks), and scheduled events crush IV (Budget day VIX -9% avg over 15 yrs; post-earnings/event crush). A static long-variance strip pays this premium continuously and is hit by the sigma*vega/2 vol-collapse loss. Truncation worsens the asymmetry: full strip cost paid, capped upside convexity. So gate to anticipated fat-tail windows (genuine binary events like general elections), NEVER hold through scheduled crushes (Budget, mostly RBI holds), and never buy-and-hold.

net_actionable: Do NOT attempt a true 1/K^2 variance-swap strip at NSE retail; instead, when you specifically want spot-robust long gamma into a fat-tail event (e.g. a general election), build a deliberately-truncated 'poor-man's log contract': a put-heavy 3-5 strike NIFTY weekly portfolio with leg sizes approximating 1/K^2 (more lots on lower strikes), delta-hedged with NIFTY futures, and accept it is an APPROXIMATION (short the deep tail), not a variance swap. Backtest the convexity vs a single ATM straddle in a custom-data QC harness (self-computed Greeks + custom FeeModel charging Rs 20/order PER LEG + 0.10% sell-side STT) before risking capital, and gate entry to anticipated tail windows only.

nse_qc_implementation: Instruments: NIFTY 50 weekly options (Tuesday expiry), 3-5 strikes — e.g. ATM straddle plus 1-2 OTM put legs at ~90-95% and ~85% of spot, leg lot-counts scaled up toward lower strikes to crudely mimic 1/K^2 (most weight on low-strike puts -> ~60% puts). Delta hedge with the front-month NIFTY future (~Rs 16-17 lakh/lot), rebalanced daily. Expiries: weekly, rolled each Tuesday for a constant-maturity approximation (each roll re-pays per-leg brokerage). DATA/RESEARCH path on QC: no native NSE options/F&O data — ingest custom NSE option records as a custom data type; compute Greeks/IV yourself with QuantLib or py_vollib; attach a custom FeeModel (subclass FeeModel.GetOrderFee) charging Rs 20/order per leg + 0.10% sell-side premium STT (since 1-Oct-2024) + ~0.03553% exchange + 18% GST on brokerage+exchange+SEBI + 0.003% buy-side stamp, plus a custom FillModel/SlippageModel for wide wing spreads; run delta-hedging logic in code. Benchmark: compare the truncated strip's P&L through a simulated 5-6% gap (election-style) against a single ATM straddle of equal vega to confirm the constant-gamma / convex-tail advantage actually survives truncation and the per-leg cost stack.
resolved_tension: Claim (clean spot-robust variance via 1/K^2 strip + convex tail bonus) is mathematically right; counterclaims (illiquid/untruncatable wings, continuous-hedge requirement, sigma*vega/2 collapse loss) are not edge cases but the binding constraints for retail. They net out adversely: the very feature that justifies the strip's complexity — convex payoff on large moves — is delivered by the deep-OTM wings, which at NSE are illiquid, lot-quantized, and savaged by flat Rs 20/leg brokerage, so the buyable strip is precisely the strip with its tail convexity removed. Layer on positive VRP (~75% of days) making a static long-variance position negative-carry, and the honest conclusion is: keep the INSIGHT (constant dollar-gamma is a real, useful property and variance>vol on big moves), but DROP the pretense of replicating a variance swap. Recast to a small, event-gated, deliberately-truncated put-heavy long-gamma approximation that is acknowledged to be short the far tail.

refute.why: The math is standard and correct. A daily-rebalanced fixed-leverage product tracks L_t/L_0 ≈ (S_t/S_0)^λ · exp(-0.5·λ(λ-1)σ²t); the exponential is a deterministic, monotone-decreasing-in-σ² short-realized-variance term for λ>1 AND for λ<0 (λ=2 and λ=-1 both give coefficient -σ²t). The discrete '(1+x)(1-x)<1' is its origin. The Kelly point is also correct: log-growth g(λ)=λμ-0.5λ²σ² peaks at λ*=μ/σ², so any fixed λ is an over-bet that worsens exactly as σ² rises — the mirror of when long gamma pays. The sharpest and fully-correct sub-claim is convexity-in-price ≠ long-variance: a long/short-ETF or 'looks-like-a-straddle' combo can be net SHORT realized variance. The counterclaim (Sinclair p.232-233: the naive-leverage power term λ·drift can dominate the drag, so the product can OVERPERFORM) does NOT refute the variance sign — it only bounds NET P&L. It would refute a strawman ('leveraged ETFs always bleed'), which the candidate does not assert: the candidate says they bleed IN CHOP (drift≈0 → only the negative variance term survives), which is correct. Ch.8 fat-tails actually reinforces the warning (true optimal leverage is below λ*=μ/σ²).

nse.why: No direct NSE trade exists. Web-confirmed (Dec-2025/2026 sources) and consistent with the fact base: SEBI permits NO listed leveraged/inverse equity ETFs on NSE/BSE; the only 2x India product (Direxion INDL) is US-listed and not accessible to domestic retail via Zerodha/Samco/QC. India VIX is untradable (futures dead ~Feb-2016; no options/ETF/ETN ever). So a retail trader literally cannot be silently short vol via a domestic leveraged ETP, and cannot trade the drag directly. Two transferable pieces survive: (1) the CAUTION — do not equate payoff-convexity with long gamma; recognize any embedded daily-reset/path-dependent leverage is short realized variance (note: intraday MIS margin is NOT a daily-NAV-reset product, so the ETF compounding-drag does NOT transfer to MIS — it is plain margin). (2) The Carr-Madan spanning identity IS reusable on NIFTY's liquid OTM chain: a 1/K²-weighted strip of OTM NIFTY options synthesizes a long-realized-variance (variance-swap-like) payoff — the constructive opposite of the ETF drag. BUT the cost stack truncates it: far-OTM tails cost 13-30%+ round-trip (flat Rs 40 brokerage swamps Rs 2-5 premium), so a full-fidelity variance strip is brokerage-prohibitive for retail; only a truncated few-strike version is feasible, which reintroduces strike risk and is no longer a clean variance swap. QC has no NSE options data and no F&O routing, so any test needs custom data + self-computed Greeks, or a pivot to US SPX/SPY/VIX where leveraged ETFs and the genuine drag actually exist.

regime.why: The caution MATTERS exactly in high-σ²/choppy/mean-reverting regimes (drift≈0 → only the negative variance term bites) — the same regimes where long gamma pays; it is irrelevant in strong-trend low-chop regimes where the naive-leverage term dominates and a leveraged structure overperforms. For the constructive flip (NIFTY long-variance strip): it is LONG realized variance, so it bleeds via theta/VRP in calm contango (India VRP positive ~75% of days, mean ~+1.2 vol pts; ~25% inversion) and pays in vol-expansion/backwardation (elections Jun-4-2024 VIX +27.7% close, intraday ~31; COVID-Mar-2020). Gating: deploy long-variance replication only when implied is cheap on the regime gauge (India VIX low in its post-2014 ~13-17 band) and/or into genuine fat-tail events (general elections — NOT budget/RBI, which reliably CRUSH IV: 15/15 budget days down avg >9%). Sizing: honor λ*=μ/σ² — do not scale up the long-vol strip when σ² is already high (you'd overpay premium); fat tails argue for sub-Kelly sizing.

net_actionable: Never treat a payoff that merely looks convex/straddle-like as long volatility — verify the SIGN of realized-variance exposure (long gamma = pays in chop; short variance = bleeds in chop). On NSE this is a screening discipline, not a trade: there is no domestic leveraged/inverse ETP to trade or to be caught short-vol by. When you DO want long realized variance, build it explicitly as a truncated 1/K²-weighted strip of liquid OTM NIFTY weekly/monthly options (the Carr-Madan constructive flip of the ETF drag), gated to cheap-IV/pre-fat-tail-event regimes.
conditions: No domestic leveraged/inverse ETF or India VIX ETP exists on NSE — treat this insight as a sign-checking discipline plus a NIFTY-options construction, never as an ETP trade.
Intraday MIS leverage does NOT carry the daily-NAV-reset compounding drag (it is plain margin, not a rebalanced fund) — do not misapply the ETF math to it.

A full Carr-Madan variance strip is cost-prohibitive at retail (far-OTM tails 13-30%+ round-trip due to flat Rs 40 brokerage); use only a truncated few-strike approximation and accept residual strike risk.
Long-variance construction is short the positive VRP (~+1.2 vol pts, positive ~75% of days) — only deploy when IV is cheap and/or into genuine fat-tail events; avoid budget/RBI days where IV crushes.

Any QC backtest requires custom NSE options data + self-computed Greeks (no native chain/Greeks/F&O routing); the validated-engine path is to prototype on US SPX/SPY/VIX, where leveraged ETFs and the literal drag exist.
nse_qc_implementation: No direct instrument. CONCEPTUAL screen: decompose every prospective 'long-vol' structure to its daily realized-variance sign before sizing. CONSTRUCTIVE long-variance proxy on NSE: truncated 1/K²-weighted strip of OTM NIFTY options — e.g. 5-9 strikes spanning ~±3 to ±8% around spot on the nearest liquid NIFTY weekly (Tuesday expiry, NIFTY-only post-Nov-2024; lot 65 Jan-2026 series) or monthly, quantity_i ∝ ΔK_i/K_i², capped to liquid strikes to keep brokerage tolerable; this is long realized variance, the opposite of the ETF drag. DATA/EXECUTION: QC has no NSE options data and no F&O routing — ingest custom NIFTY option records, compute Greeks/IV via QuantLib/py_vollib, attach custom FeeModel (Rs 20/order + 0.10% sell-side STT + 0.03553% exchange/side + 18% GST + 0.003% stamp buy-side) and custom Fill/Slippage; live via Kite Connect outside LEAN. To study the LITERAL leveraged-ETF drag with a turnkey engine, pivot to US: AlgoSeek US Index Options (SPX/VIX from Jan-2012) plus real leveraged ETFs (SSO/UPRO/SH/SDS), fully native in QC.
resolved_tension: Claim and counterclaim net cleanly because they address different objects. The claim is about the SIGN of the variance term (always negative/short-variance for λ>1 or λ<0, exact and deterministic) and is unconditionally true. The counterclaim is about NET P&L of the product (the (S_t/S_0)^λ drift/power term can dominate the drag in strong trends, so the fund can overperform). Both are right: the drag is always present and is what makes leveraged structures bleed in chop, but it is not always the dominant effect on total return. The candidate is careful — it asserts hidden short-variance and bleed-in-chop, not perpetual bleeding — so it survives the counterclaim intact. Net: the insight is true and conceptually valuable; it simply has no tradable NSE instrument, so it recasts into (a) a sign-checking screen and (b) the Carr-Madan long-variance NIFTY construction.
citations: Sinclair Vol Trading 2e Ch.13 p.231-237 (volatility drag = short-variance term; fixed leverage as Kelly over-bet, critical λ*=μ/σ²; leveraged ETF as perpetual power option)
Sinclair Ch.13 p.234-235 (long/short-ETF replicates a straddle that is SHORT realized variance — convexity-in-price ≠ long variance)
